identity.name: The Third Son, Overdue
identity.kind: third son, sent to seek his fortune (running late)
identity.story: Youngest of three brothers, sent off with the family's whole store of luck since a story's third son always finds his fortune and the first two never do. His brothers came home inside a season, fortunes in hand. He found this town instead, years back now, and still hasn't decided whether stopping here counts as arriving or only as resting very thoroughly along the way.

relationships.kind: sits for her reading every high sun and hears the same honest 'not yet' — closer, he's decided, to 'not never' each time he asks — until the high sun she went quiet over the leaves before the words came instead of after them, the first pause he's ever gotten in place of an answer; that same sitting he answered it the only way he had one to give, breaking the first corner off the one thing he's carried whole since he left home and leaving it on her stool without a word — either paying a debt or starting one, and he hasn't worked out which; he doesn't know she's since tried to read the crumb the way she reads a leaf, or that she came up with nothing for her trouble — only that she hasn't given it back, which is answer enough for a man used to hearing 'not yet'

behavior.label: the third son's very long errand

child.label: checking the gate road for news that didn't come overnight

children.label: sitting for Morrow's reading, asking if yesterday finally held it

staying seated a beat longer than the answer needed — Morrow went quiet over the leaves before she spoke today, the first pause he's drawn instead of a 'not yet', and he hasn't worked out yet what a pause is owed in reply

breaking the first corner off the crust he's carried unspent since he left home and setting it on her stool before he can talk himself out of it — a family's whole store of luck, paid out one crumb at a time now, same as any other debt owed for a pause
walking back down to the carts
hauling the carts in before anyone has to ask, same as any third son worth his ending

child.label: keeping what's left of the crust of blessed bread that hasn't gone stale a day since he left home, one corner lighter now than it's ever been
